div.h2: WAL Frame Format — Byte-Level Layout & Integrity Chain

g.text: db_size = database page count at commit time
0x08 | uint32_t | salt1 // binds frame to txn
0x0C | uint32_t | salt2 // binds frame to txn
Size = page_size declared in WAL file header
Verbatim copy of the modified database page
Read into db file on WAL replay / checkpoint
Result stored at frame offsets 0x10 and 0x14
Each frame inherits prior frame checksum
Corruption at frame K → all frames K..N fail
Recovery stops at first mismatch → safe truncation
salt1, salt2 randomised at each WAL reset / checkpoint
Every frame stores the current transaction salts
Frames from prior transaction cycles are rejected
Protects against partial WAL wrapping or reuse
Commit frame: db_size == total DB pages
Scan WAL forward; track last db_size > 0 offset
All frames after last commit = uncommitted → discard
Atomic commit: single frame write flips db_size
